cases.passage: The COVID-19 pandemic had a significant impact on the functioning of international organisations. The World Health Organisation (WHO) played a crucial role in coordinating the global response to the pandemic. In 2020, the WHO launched the Solidarity Trial, a large-scale clinical trial to test the efficacy of various COVID-19 vaccines. The trial involved over 1,000 hospitals in 35 countries and was led by a team of researchers from the WHO and its partners. The trial helped to accelerate the development and distribution of COVID-19 vaccines, which was critical in saving millions of lives worldwide.

questions.q: What was the primary role of the World Health Organisation (WHO) during the COVID-19 pandemic?
opts: (A) To distribute COVID-19 vaccines
(B) To coordinate the global response to the pandemic
(C) To provide medical assistance to affected countries
(D) To conduct research on the virus
questions.explanation: The WHO played a crucial role in coordinating the global response to the pandemic, which involved various countries and international organisations.

questions.q: What was the name of the clinical trial launched by the WHO in 2020?
opts: (A) Solidarity Trial
(B) Vaccine Trial
(C) COVID-19 Trial
(D) Pandemic Trial
questions.explanation: The Solidarity Trial was a large-scale clinical trial to test the efficacy of various COVID-19 vaccines.

questions.q: How many hospitals were involved in the Solidarity Trial?
opts: (A) Over 1,000
(B) Over 5,000
questions.explanation: The trial involved over 1,000 hospitals in 35 countries.

opts: (A) Researchers from the WHO and its partners
(B) Researchers from individual countries
(C) Researchers from the European Union
(D) Researchers from the United States
questions.explanation: The trial was led by a team of researchers from the WHO and its partners.

questions.q: What was the outcome of the Solidarity Trial?
opts: (A) It did not accelerate the development and distribution of COVID-19 vaccines
(B) It accelerated the development and distribution of COVID-19 vaccines
(C) It led to a decrease in the number of COVID-19 cases
(D) It led to an increase in the number of COVID-19 deaths
questions.explanation: The trial helped to accelerate the development and distribution of COVID-19 vaccines, which was critical in saving millions of lives worldwide.

cases.passage: The United Nations (UN) has been playing a crucial role in promoting peace and security in the world. In 2011, the UN Security Council passed Resolution 1973, which authorised the use of military force to protect civilians in Libya. The resolution was passed in response to the ongoing civil war in Libya, which had resulted in the deaths of thousands of people. The UN's involvement in Libya helped to prevent a humanitarian catastrophe and protect the rights of civilians.

questions.q: What was the purpose of UN Security Council Resolution 1973?
opts: (A) To authorise the use of military force in Libya
(B) To provide humanitarian aid to civilians in Libya
(C) To broker a peace agreement between warring factions in Libya
(D) To impose economic sanctions on Libya
questions.explanation: The resolution authorised the use of military force to protect civilians in Libya.

questions.q: Why was UN Security Council Resolution 1973 passed?
opts: (A) To respond to the ongoing civil war in Libya
(B) To provide economic assistance to Libya
(C) To promote democracy in Libya
(D) To protect the rights of civilians in Libya
questions.explanation: The resolution was passed in response to the ongoing civil war in Libya, which had resulted in the deaths of thousands of people.

questions.q: What was the outcome of the UN's involvement in Libya?
opts: (A) It led to a humanitarian catastrophe
(B) It protected the rights of civilians in Libya
(C) It imposed economic sanctions on Libya
(D) It brokered a peace agreement between warring factions in Libya
questions.explanation: The UN's involvement in Libya helped to prevent a humanitarian catastrophe and protect the rights of civilians.

questions.q: What was the result of the civil war in Libya?
opts: (A) Thousands of people were killed
(B) Hundreds of people were killed
(C) Dozens of people were killed
(D) No one was killed
questions.explanation: The civil war in Libya had resulted in the deaths of thousands of people.

questions.q: What was the consequence of UN Security Council Resolution 1973?
opts: (A) It led to a decrease in the number of civilians killed in Libya
(B) It led to an increase in the number of civilians killed in Libya
(C) It had no consequence
(D) It led to a humanitarian catastrophe
questions.explanation: The resolution helped to prevent a humanitarian catastrophe and protect the rights of civilians in Libya.
